name: Adjacent vardenafil study found no prominent cognitive effect in healthy adults
statement: In healthy adults, the adjacent PDE5 inhibitor vardenafil did not show prominent cognitive performance benefits in the PubMed abstract; participants made more mistakes on a reaction-time task after 20 mg vardenafil and reported headache and weakness more often.
population_or_context: Healthy adults; adjacent drug-class evidence rather than tadalafil evidence.
time_horizon: Acute oral vardenafil treatment session; exact timing not extracted from abstract.
quantitative_note: No numeric effect sizes extracted from abstract.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract states no prominent cognition effects, more mistakes on reaction-time task after 20 mg, and more headache/weakness.

caveats: The extraction is abstract-only and needs full-text verification.
It supports caution about PDE5 class cognitive-enhancement claims, not a tadalafil-specific conclusion.
population_profile: Healthy young adults; sex distribution and age range not extracted from abstract.
tadalafil_exposure: No tadalafil; vardenafil 10-20 mg oral treatment.
endpoint_measure: Memory, executive functioning, EEG, simple reaction time, and complaint questionnaires.
effect_estimate: No prominent cognitive effects in abstract; more mistakes on simple reaction-time task after 20 mg vardenafil.
safety_findings: Headache and feeling weak were reported more after vardenafil treatment in the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Healthy-adult population is relevant, but drug exposure is not tadalafil; therefore directness is low for tadalafil in healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Abstract-only extraction; adjacent drug-class inference; full trial methods and exact participant characteristics not verified.
class_transfer_limits: Adjacent vardenafil evidence only; do not transfer to tadalafil without accounting for different molecule, pharmacokinetics, dose, exposure timing, PDE selectivity, endpoint, and source-depth limits.
